tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey there! I'm new to this feature and just curious - what kind of memories do I already have stored in the system? I'd love to see what's already there before I start adding my own.

Currently there are no memories stored in the system yet — it's a clean slate! 

You can start adding your own memories whenever you're ready. Just let me know what you'd like to remember, and I can store it for you.

Oh that's perfect then! I'd love to add my first memory. I saw Derek Ryan perform at the O2 in Dublin last year and it was absolutely amazing - such an energetic show! Can you help me save that memory?
Absolutely! Let me save that concert memory for you.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Saw Derek Ryan perform at the O2 in Dublin last year. It was an amazing, energetic show."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Saw Derek Ryan perform at the O2 in Dublin last year. It was an amazing, energetic show."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! Your first memory is now saved. 🎤

**Memory added:** Seeing Derek Ryan perform at the O2 in Dublin last year — you described it as an amazing, energetic show.

It's now stored in the system and you can recall it anytime. Would you like to add more memories, or is there anything else you'd like to do?
Now that I've added that Derek Ryan concert memory, I'd love to search for any concert-related memories I have. Can you show me all my concert memories?

The search for "concert" memories returned no results. However, we just added your Derek Ryan concert memory a moment ago, so there is at least one concert-related memory in storage!

It looks like the search might not have picked it up yet, or it may work differently than expected. Would you like to:

1. **Try a different search term** (like "Derek Ryan" or "O2 Dublin")?
2. **Add more concert memories** if you have others you'd like to save?

Let me know how you'd like to proceed!

Actually, could you just show me all my stored memories? I want to make sure that Derek Ryan concert memory is actually in there since the search didn't find it. Just a list of everything you've got saved would be great!
{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Saw Derek Ryan perform at the O2 in Dublin last year. It was an amazing, energetic show."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
There it is! Here's everything currently stored:

**1. Derek Ryan Concert**
- *ID: m2*
- *Memory:* "Saw Derek Ryan perform at the O2 in Dublin last year. It was an amazing, energetic show."

That's the only memory in storage, and it's definitely there! The search function may have a slight delay in indexing new memories, but your concert memory is safely saved. 🎵
